mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5 percent month-over-month reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization, generating positive equity returns when inflation remains below 3 percent.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 0.5 percent coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in greater than 60 percent of low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 58 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation accelerating above 3.0 percent forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 62 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and positive 20-day SPX returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields negative 20-day SPX returns in greater than 57 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales contraction below zero percent during a high-rate environment confirms demand weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during high-rate periods produce positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.
